state.title: bench-targets/react-state/useCartState.test.mjs

part.text: I can see the bug. In `useCartState.mjs` (line 6), `addItem` creates `next = state`, which means both variables reference the same object. Then `next.items.push(item)` mutates the original state object, violating the immutability expectation in the test.

state.title: Run test to verify the fix works

part.text:
**Bug:** In `useCartState.mjs`, `addItem` mutated the original state by reusing the same object reference and calling `.push()` directly on it.

**Fix:** Changed lines 6-9 in `bench-targets/react-state/src/useCartState.mjs` to create a new object with a spread array instead of mutating the existing one.

**Verification:** Test passed - all assertions confirmed the fix works.

```
pass 1
fail 0
```
